pr.title: GitHub PR metadata backfill: batch import + canonical PR sequence ledger
pr.description:
## Summary

Backfills the full GitHub PR history of this repo (67 PRs) into the GitHub Metadata Import Framework and reconciles the two PR numbering sequences into one canonical, durable reference — before the divergence gets messier.

Why: PR numbering restarted with this account (GitHub #1 == canonical "PR #112" from the prior 112-PR sequence). Recent PRs (#48–#67) stopped encoding a canonical number in their titles, so cross-account referencing is already breaking down. This PR gives the other worker account a complete, machine-readable base to populate the knowledge graph from.

What's in it:

- `GitHubMetadataImportEngine.backfill(payload_dir)` — batch import of `*.json` payloads. Canonical number resolution: payload `global_capability_number` → else parsed from title via `^(?:Integration\s+)?PR\s*#(\d+)` (`canonical_number_from_title`) → else recorded as an explicit **canonical gap** (never invented). Duplicate imports are skipped, not fatal, so the backfill is safely re-runnable.
- `GitHubMetadataImportEngine.generate_sequence_ledger()` — renders `docs/logs/pr-sequence-ledger.md`: one row per PR, `Canonical # | GitHub PR # | Title | Status | Merged at | Author`, sorted by GitHub PR number, gaps marked `— (gap)`.
- New CLI `axiom github-import-backfill --payload-dir <dir> [--ledger-out <path>] [--json-output]`, cataloged in the command registry (READ_ONLY / SAFE) and added to `EXPECTED_AXIOM_COMMANDS`.
- `scripts/fetch_github_pr_metadata.py` — the only network-touching piece (via `gh api`), writes per-PR payloads (PR + commits + files + labels) in the engine's shape. The framework itself stays network-free per its doctrine.
- Committed evidence: `artifacts/github_pr_history/pr-0001..0067.json` (raw payloads) and the generated `docs/logs/pr-sequence-ledger.md`.

Backfill result: 67 imported, 0 failed, 23 canonical gaps — GitHub #9, #20, #38 (skill/fix PRs that never had a canonical number) and #48–#67 (the recent run where canonical titling stopped). Assigning continuation numbers (158+) to the gap PRs is an operator decision, deliberately not automated.

What did NOT change: `import_metadata` semantics, registry/timeline shapes, schema version, existing evidence outputs.

Tests (tier 2, targeted): `tests/test_github_metadata_import.py` 54 passed (6 new: canonical parsing, backfill import/derivation, re-runnability, malformed payload, missing dir, ledger ordering/gaps), `tests/test_command_registry.py` 27 passed; ruff clean on changed files. Skill `testing-cli-governance` updated in the same PR.

Link to Devin session: https://app.devin.ai/sessions/8ad1c9ac0d2f4252bb77647f1eabaac7
Requested by: @plamen-dev
pr.author: plamen-dev

GitHub PR metadata backfill: batch import + canonical PR sequence ledger

Co-Authored-By: Devin AI <158243242+devin-ai-integration[bot]@users.noreply.github.com>

commits.author: plamen-dev
commits.message:
Knowledge graph auto-ingest: capability-graph-ingest command (C10) (#69)

Scope

Adds a read-only graph auto-ingest path that converts existing Axiom evidence and metadata artifacts into a capability knowledge graph without requiring manual capability-graph-create payloads.

Key Changes

- Adds `GraphAutoIngestEngine` to scan existing artifact sources and assemble a capability graph through the existing `CapabilityKnowledgeGraphEngine`.
- Adds the new CLI command `capability-graph-ingest` with `--artifacts-root` and `--json-output` options.
- Catalogs `capability-graph-ingest` in the command registry as READ_ONLY / SAFE.
- Ingests evidence intake reports as validation nodes linked to shared capability nodes.
- Ingests execution-chain evidence as artifact nodes linked to capabilities.
- Ingests validation evidence runs as validation nodes with run status and command-count metadata.
- Ingests GitHub metadata import reports as PR/event nodes carrying repository PR number and global capability number.
- Uses deterministic node and edge IDs derived from source artifact IDs so repeated ingests over the same inputs produce stable graph structure.
- Records malformed artifact files as skipped instead of failing the ingest.
- Preserves upstream artifact files without mutation.
- Updates CLI governance testing skill guidance for the new graph-ingest command.

Validation

- Ran targeted tier-2 tests:
  - `tests/test_graph_auto_ingest.py`
  - `tests/test_command_registry.py`
- Result:
  - `tests/test_graph_auto_ingest.py`: 10 passed.
  - `tests/test_command_registry.py`: 27 passed.
- Covered all-source ingest, shared capability-node deduplication, deterministic reruns, malformed artifact skipping, no upstream mutation, and canonical/global PR number carry-through.
- Ruff clean.
- Real-data run on repo artifacts produced:
  - 76 nodes
  - 6 edges
  - 0 skipped
- PR is stacked on #68 and should merge after #68 so GitHub can retarget it cleanly to main.

Non-Goals

- Does not change `CapabilityKnowledgeGraphEngine` semantics.
- Does not change graph schemas.
- Does not mutate upstream evidence, validation, execution-chain, or GitHub metadata artifacts.
- Does not replace existing evidence producers.
- Does not create a new graph framework.
- Does not make graph ingestion a promotion or confidence mutation path.
- Does not require Revit or Revit validation.
- Does not directly modify canonical documents.

Architectural Significance

This PR turns Axiom’s existing evidence and PR metadata artifacts into graph-consumable knowledge without manual graph construction. It closes an important evidence-flow gap: artifacts that were previously inspectable but disconnected can now feed a deterministic, read-only capability graph. This strengthens the path toward Atlas and future operator cockpit views by giving them a structured graph substrate sourced from existing evidence, validation, and PR metadata while preserving local-first and non-mutating safety boundaries.

Co-authored-by: plamen.hristov@imsdevlab.onmicrosoft.com <plamen.m.hristov@gmail.com>
